matter.title: Commercial property sale: Portland Gateway Industrial, Biddeford — Grace C. Roe to Maria Q. Fairbanks

jurisdiction.court_type: Maine Registry of Deeds / private closing

matter.summary: Maria Q. Fairbanks, through Bayview Property Partners LLC, is acquiring Portland Gateway Industrial (flex/industrial warehouse) at 500 Commercial Street, Biddeford from Grace C. Roe for $22970515 — roughly a 6.25% cap on $443356 of NOI — with closing set for 2026-04-19.

fact_pattern.summary: Maria Q. Fairbanks, through Bayview Property Partners LLC, is acquiring Portland Gateway Industrial (flex/industrial warehouse) at 500 Commercial Street, Biddeford from Grace C. Roe for $22970515 — roughly a 6.25% cap on $443356 of NOI — with closing set for 2026-04-19.
fact_pattern.narrative:
Grace C. Roe has agreed to sell Portland Gateway Industrial, a 91434-square-foot flex/industrial warehouse at 500 Commercial Street in Biddeford, Sagadahoc County, to Maria Q. Fairbanks's acquisition entity, Bayview Property Partners LLC, for $22970515. The pricing reflects a 6.25% capitalization rate on trailing NOI of about $443356, with the property 94% occupied across 12 tenant(s), anchored by a regional credit union on a 10-year NNN lease.

The PSA became effective 2026-01-19 with a $265963 deposit and a due-diligence period running to 2026-03-02. Buyer's diligence covers title and the ALTA/NSPS survey with Table A items 1-4, 6-11, and 16, a Phase I ESA, tenant estoppels and SNDAs, service contracts, and the rent roll and CAM reconciliations. The Phase I reported historic fill and a closed UST with a no-further-action letter; all tenant estoppels returned clean.

Buyer is financing with a bridge loan with a 24-month term of about $13632690 at roughly 70% LTV, subject to a 1.25x debt-service-coverage test, with the financing contingency running to 2026-03-24. Counsel Ingrid B. Kingsley of Hawthorne Legal Group is negotiating the deed, title endorsements, estoppel and SNDA forms, and the closing statement for a 2026-04-19 closing.
undisputed_facts: Portland Gateway Industrial at 500 Commercial Street, Biddeford is under contract from Grace C. Roe to Bayview Property Partners LLC at $22970515.
Trailing NOI is about $443356 at a 6.25% cap rate.
The due-diligence period runs to 2026-03-02 and closing is set for 2026-04-19.
disputed_facts: Whether the Phase I finding (historic fill and a closed UST with a no-further-action letter) requires a Phase II, a price adjustment, or seller indemnity.
Resolution of the estoppel issue: all tenant estoppels returned clean.
Whether the loan clears the 1.25x DSCR test at the agreed price.

third_parties.name: Summit Outfitters Inc.
third_parties.relevance: Manages the tenants and provided the rent roll and CAM reconciliations.

third_parties.name: John W. Poe
third_parties.relevance: Performed the Phase I Environmental Site Assessment.

third_parties.name: Cobblestone Partners Co.
third_parties.relevance: Issues an ALTA owner's and lender's policy with endorsements.

third_parties.name: Downeast Federal Savings
third_parties.relevance: Underwrites the acquisition loan against NOI and DSCR.

third_parties.name: Harbor Logistics Co.
third_parties.relevance: Marketed the property and brokered the transaction.

exchanges.question: What are you buying?
exchanges.answer: Portland Gateway Industrial, a 91434 SF flex/industrial warehouse at 500 Commercial Street, for $22970515.

exchanges.question: How did you price it?
exchanges.answer: About a 6.25% cap on $443356 NOI; it's 94% leased across 12 tenants, anchored by a regional credit union on a 10-year NNN lease.

exchanges.question: Who takes title?
exchanges.answer: My acquisition entity, Bayview Property Partners LLC.

exchanges.question: What's your diligence plan?
exchanges.answer: Title and an ALTA survey, a Phase I ESA, estoppels and SNDAs, service contracts, and the rent roll and CAM true-ups — all by 2026-03-02.

exchanges.question: Any environmental concerns?
exchanges.answer: The Phase I found historic fill and a closed UST with a no-further-action letter; we're deciding whether we need a Phase II.

exchanges.question: How is it financed?
exchanges.answer: a bridge loan with a 24-month term at about 70% LTV; the lender wants 1.25x coverage. Contingency runs to 2026-03-24.

goals: Close on 2026-04-19 at $22970515 with clean title and required endorsements.
Resolve the Phase I finding (historic fill and a closed UST with a no-further-action letter) with a Phase II, credit, or indemnity as warranted.
Obtain clean tenant estoppels and lender-required SNDAs.
priorities: Loan commitment meeting the 1.25x DSCR test.
desired_outcomes: A funded acquisition into Bayview Property Partners LLC with owner's and lender's title policies.
non_starters: Closing over an unquantified environmental liability.
Waiving financing before a firm commitment.

constraints.budget: Equity plus a bridge loan with a 24-month term of about $13632690.

sub_questions: Do estoppels confirm rent, term, and no defaults?
Is the CAM reconciliation dispute material?

issues.issue: Satisfaction of the DSCR-based financing contingency
sub_questions: Does NOI support 1.25x coverage at the loan amount?

issues.issue: Maine real estate transfer tax on the sale

sub_questions: Are there survey encroachments or access issues?
Which endorsements does the lender require?
Does the Phase I trigger a Phase II?
Is a bona fide prospective purchaser defense preserved?

evidence.title: Lender term sheet and commitment
evidence.description: a bridge loan with a 24-month term at 70% LTV and 1.25x DSCR.

evidence.title: Tenant estoppel certificates
evidence.description: Signed estoppels; all tenant estoppels returned clean.

evidence.title: Phase I Environmental Site Assessment
evidence.description: The consultant's ESA reporting historic fill and a closed UST with a no-further-action letter.

amounts.label: Acquisition loan
amounts.basis: a bridge loan with a 24-month term at 70% LTV.

amounts.label: Earnest money deposit
amounts.basis: Held by the title company as escrow agent.

amounts.label: Estimated Maine transfer tax
amounts.basis: $2.20 per $500 of consideration, split between the parties.

communications.subject: Signed LOI — Portland Gateway Industrial, Biddeford
communications.summary: Broker circulates the executed term sheet and opens due diligence.
communications.body: Attached is the fully-signed LOI for Portland Gateway Industrial at $22970515 (~6.25% cap on $443356 NOI). Deposit is $265963, due diligence runs to 2026-03-02, and target closing is 2026-04-19. Seller will deliver the rent roll, leases, service contracts, and environmental history to the data room this week.

communications.from: Ingrid B. Kingsley, Hawthorne Legal Group
communications.subject: Due diligence request list — Portland Gateway Industrial
communications.summary: Buyer's counsel sends the diligence checklist.
communications.body: On behalf of Bayview Property Partners LLC, please post to the data room: the current rent roll and trailing-12 operating statements, all leases and amendments, CAM reconciliations, the title policy and existing survey, environmental reports, service and management contracts, and any notices of violation. We will order a Phase I ESA, an ALTA/NSPS survey, and tenant estoppels.

communications.subject: Phase I ESA results — 500 Commercial Street
communications.summary: Consultant reports the Phase I finding.
communications.body: Our Phase I for 500 Commercial Street is complete. The assessment identified historic fill and a closed UST with a no-further-action letter. We can scope a limited Phase II if you want to quantify any impact before the due-diligence deadline; otherwise the report supports a bona fide prospective purchaser position with continuing obligations.

communications.from: Ingrid B. Kingsley, Hawthorne Legal Group
communications.subject: Estoppels and CAM — Portland Gateway Industrial
communications.summary: Buyer's counsel raises the estoppel/CAM issue.
communications.body: Reviewing the returned certificates, all tenant estoppels returned clean. Please have the property manager reconcile the anchor tenant's CAM before closing and confirm no landlord defaults. We also need lender-form SNDAs from the top tenants to satisfy our financing.

communications.subject: Loan commitment — Portland Gateway Industrial
communications.summary: Lender issues a commitment subject to the DSCR test.
communications.body: We are pleased to commit a bridge loan with a 24-month term of $13632690 at approximately 70% LTV and 7.10%, subject to the property underwriting to a minimum 1.25x debt-service coverage on stabilized NOI, satisfactory estoppels and SNDAs, and a clean environmental. Please confirm resolution of the Phase I item before funding.

communications.subject: Closing statement — Portland Gateway Industrial, Biddeford
communications.summary: Escrow agent circulates the settlement statement and recording plan.
communications.body: Closing for Portland Gateway Industrial is set for 2026-04-19. The statement reflects the $22970515 price, $265963 deposit credit, prorated rents and CAM, tenant security deposits transferred to Bayview Property Partners LLC, the acquisition loan of $13632690, and the Maine transfer tax split between the parties. We will issue the ALTA owner's and lender's policies and record the deed, mortgage, and RETTD upon funding.

facts.largest_tenant: a regional credit union on a 10-year NNN lease
facts.financing_type: a bridge loan with a 24-month term
facts.environmental_finding: historic fill and a closed UST with a no-further-action letter
facts.title_survey: ALTA/NSPS survey with Table A items 1-4, 6-11, and 16
facts.deed_type: warranty deed with commercial closing affidavit
